description: This file controls how the game controls the area levels, including how the level is built, what rules are allowed on the level, and what monsters/objects can spawn on the level.
source: Colonnes du .txt (BKVince 3.2) ; types/descriptions du guide TXT eezstreet-d2rdoc.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: Defines the unique name pointer for the area level, which is used in other files

columns.name: Id
columns.description: Defines the unique numeric ID for the area level, which is used in other files

columns.name: Pal
columns.description: Defines which palette file to use for the area level. This uses index values from 0 to 4 to convey Act 1 to Act 5.

columns.name: Act
columns.description: Defines the Act number that the area level is a part of. This uses index values from 0 to 4 to convey Act 1 to Act 5.

columns.name: QuestFlag
columns.description: Controls what quest record that the player needs to have completed before being allowed to enter this area level, while playing in Classic Mode. Each quest can have multiple quest records, and this field is looking for a specific quest record from a quest.

columns.name: QuestFlagEx
columns.description: Controls what quest record that the player needs to have completed before being allowed to enter this area level, while playing in Expansion Mode. Each quest can have multiple quest records, and this field is looking for a specific quest record from a quest.

columns.name: Layer
columns.description: Defines a unique numeric ID that is used to identify which Automap data belongs to which area level when saving and loading data from the character save.

columns.name: SizeX
columns.description: Specifies the Length tile size values of an entire area level, which are used for determining how to build the level, for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively.

columns.name: SizeY
columns.description: Specifies the Width tile size values of an entire area level, which are used for determining how to build the level, for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively.

columns.name: SizeX(N)
columns.description: Specifies the Length tile size values of an entire area level, which are used for determining how to build the level, for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively.

columns.name: SizeY(N)
columns.description: Specifies the Width tile size values of an entire area level, which are used for determining how to build the level, for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively.

columns.name: SizeX(H)
columns.description: Specifies the Length tile size values of an entire area level, which are used for determining how to build the level, for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively.

columns.name: SizeY(H)
columns.description: Specifies the Width tile size values of an entire area level, which are used for determining how to build the level, for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively.

columns.name: OffsetX
columns.description: Specifies the location X offset coordinate (measured in tile size) for the origin point of the area level in the world.

columns.name: OffsetY
columns.description: Specifies the location offset Y coordinate (measured in tile size) for the origin point of the area level in the world.

columns.name: CompletionTotalRoomsOverride
columns.description: If > 0, use this value as the total number of rooms in the level for calculating its level completion percentage. Otherwise, use the total amount of populateable rooms.

columns.name: Depend
columns.description: Assigns another level to be this area level's depended level, which controls this area level's position and how it starts building its tiles. Uses the .Id field. If this equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: Teleport
columns.description: Controls the functionality of teleport skills like Sorceress Teleport and Assassin Dragon Flight on the area level

columns.name: Rain
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then allow rain to play its effects on the area level. If the level is part of Act 5, then it will snow on the area level, instead of rain. If equals 0, then it will never rain on the area level.

columns.name: Mud
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then random bubbles will animate on the tiles that are flagged as water tiles. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: NoPer
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then allow the use of display option of Perspective Mode while the player is in the level. If equals 0, then disable the option of Perspective Mode and force the player to use Orthographic Mode while the player is in the level.

columns.name: LOSDraw
columns.description: Boolean field. If equals 1, then the level will check the player's line of sight before drawing monsters. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: FloorFilter
columns.description: Boolean field. If equals 1 and if the floor's layer in the area level equals 1, then draw the floor tiles with a linear texture sampler. If equals 0, then draw the floor tiles with a nearest texture sampler.

columns.name: BlankScreen
columns.description: Boolean field. If equals 1, then draw the area level screen. If equals 0, then do not draw the area level screen, meaning that the level will be a blank screen.

columns.name: DrawEdges
columns.description: Boolean field. If equals 1, then draw the areas in levels that are not covered by floor tiles. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: DrlgType
columns.description: Determines the type of Dynamic Random Level Generation used for building and handling different elements of the area level. Uses a numeric code to handle which type of DRLG is used.

columns.name: LevelType
columns.description: Defines the Level Type used for this area level. Uses lvltypes.Id, which is determined by what order it is defined in the lvltypes file.

columns.name: SubType
columns.description: Controls the group of tile substitutions for the area level (see lvlsub). There are defined sub types to choose from.

columns.name: SubTheme
columns.description: Controls which theme number to use in a Level Substitution (See lvlsub). The allowed values are 0 to 4, which convey which lvlsub.Prob#, lvlsub.Trials#, and lvlsub.Max# field to use. If this equals -1, then there is no sub theme for the area level.

columns.name: SubWaypoint
columns.description: Controls the level substitutions for adding waypoints in the area level (see lvlsub). This uses a defined sub type to choose from (See .SubType). This will depend on the room having a waypoint tile.

columns.name: SubShrine
columns.description: Controls the level substitutions for adding shrines in the area level (see lvlsub). This uses a defined sub type to choose from (See .SubType). This will depend on the room allowing for a shrine to spawn.

columns.name: Vis0
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis1
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis2
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis3
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis4
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis5
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis6
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Vis7
columns.description: Defines the visibility of other area levels involved with this area level, allowing for travel functionalities between levels. This uses the .Id field of another defined area level to link with this area level. If this equals 0, then no area level is specified.

columns.name: Warp0
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp1
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp2
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp3
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp4
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp5
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp6
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Warp7
columns.description: Uses the lvlwarp.Id field, which defines which Level Warp to use when exiting the area level. This is connected with the definition of the related .Vis# field. If this equals -1, then no Level Warp is specified which should also mean that the related .Vis# field is not defined.

columns.name: Intensity
columns.description: Controls the intensity value of the area level's ambient colors. This affects brightness of the room's RGB colors. Uses a value between 0 and 128. If all these related fields equal 0, then the game ignores setting the area level's ambient colors.

columns.name: Red
columns.description: Controls the red value of the area level's ambient colors. Uses a value between 0 and 255.

columns.name: Green
columns.description: Controls the green value of the area level's ambient colors. Uses a value between 0 and 255.

columns.name: Blue
columns.description: Controls the blue value of the area level's ambient colors. Uses a value between 0 and 255.

columns.name: Portal
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then this area level will be flagged as a portal level, which is saved in the player's information and can be used for keeping track of the player's portal functionalities. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: Position
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then enable special casing for positioning the player on the area level. This can mean that the player could spawn on a different location on the area level, depending on the level room's position type. An example can be when the player spawns in a town when loading the game, or using a waypoint, or using a town portal. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: SaveMonsters
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then the game will save the monsters in the area level, such as when all players leave the area level. If equals 0, then monsters will not be saved and will be removed. This is usually disabled for areas where monsters do not spawn.

columns.name: Quest
columns.description: Controls what quest record is attached to monsters that spawn in this area level. This is used for specific quests handling lists of monsters in the area level.

columns.name: WarpDist
columns.description: Defines the minimum pixel distance from a Level Warp that a monster is allowed to spawn near. Tile distance values are converted to game pixel distance values by multiplying the tile distance value by 160 / 32, where 160 is the width of pixels of a tile.

columns.name: MonLvl
columns.description: Controls the overall monster level for the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is for Classic mode only. This can affect the highest item level allowed to drop in this area level.

columns.name: MonLvl(N)
columns.description: Controls the overall monster level for the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is for Classic mode only. This can affect the highest item level allowed to drop in this area level.

columns.name: MonLvl(H)
columns.description: Controls the overall monster level for the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is for Classic mode only. This can affect the highest item level allowed to drop in this area level.

columns.name: MonLvlEx
columns.description: Controls the overall monster level for the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is for Expansion mode only. This can affect the highest item level allowed to drop in this area level.

columns.name: MonLvlEx(N)
columns.description: Controls the overall monster level for the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is for Expansion mode only. This can affect the highest item level allowed to drop in this area level.

columns.name: MonLvlEx(H)
columns.description: Controls the overall monster level for the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is for Expansion mode only. This can affect the highest item level allowed to drop in this area level.

columns.name: MonDen
columns.description: Controls the monster density on the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is a random value out of 100000, which will determine whether to spawn or not spawn a monster pack in the room of the area level. If this value equals 0, then no random monsters will populate on the area level.

columns.name: MonDen(N)
columns.description: Controls the monster density on the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is a random value out of 100000, which will determine whether to spawn or not spawn a monster pack in the room of the area level. If this value equals 0, then no random monsters will populate on the area level.

columns.name: MonDen(H)
columns.description: Controls the monster density on the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This is a random value out of 100000, which will determine whether to spawn or not spawn a monster pack in the room of the area level. If this value equals 0, then no random monsters will populate on the area level.

columns.name: MonUMin
columns.description: Defines the minimum number of Unique Monsters that can spawn in the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined.

columns.name: MonUMax
columns.description: Defines the maximum number of Unique Monsters that can spawn in the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined. Each room in the area level will attempt to spawn a Unique Monster with a 5/100 random chance, and this field's value will cap the number of successful attempts for the entire area level.

columns.name: MonUMin(N)
columns.description: Defines the minimum number of Unique Monsters that can spawn in the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined.

columns.name: MonUMax(N)
columns.description: Defines the maximum number of Unique Monsters that can spawn in the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined. Each room in the area level will attempt to spawn a Unique Monster with a 5/100 random chance, and this field's value will cap the number of successful attempts for the entire area level.

columns.name: MonUMin(H)
columns.description: Defines the minimum number of Unique Monsters that can spawn in the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined.

columns.name: MonUMax(H)
columns.description: Defines the maximum number of Unique Monsters that can spawn in the area level for Normal, Nightmare, and Hell Difficulty, respectively. This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined. Each room in the area level will attempt to spawn a Unique Monster with a 5/100 random chance, and this field's value will cap the number of successful attempts for the entire area level.

columns.name: MonWndr
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then allow Wandering Monsters to spawn on this area level (see wanderingmon). This field depends on the related .MonDen field being defined. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: MonSpcWalk
columns.description: Defines a distance value, used to handle monster pathing AI when the level has certain pathing blockers, such as jail bars or rivers. In these cases, monsters will walk randomly until a player is located within this distance value or when the monsters find a possible path to target the player. If this equals 0, then ignore this field.

columns.name: NumMon
columns.description: Controls the number of different monsters randomly spawn in the area level. The maximum value is 13. This controls the number of random selections from the 25 related .mon# and .umon# fields or .nmon# fields, depending on the game difficulty.

columns.name: mon1
columns.description: Defines which monsters can spawn on the area level for Normal Difficulty. Community note: In the legacy version of the game, only mon1-mon10 are present in the MPQ but the columns mon11-mon25 can be added to the file and work correctly. In Resurrected, all 25 columns are present in Blizzard's files.

columns.name: rangedspawn
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then for the first monster, try to pick a ranged type. If equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: cmon1
columns.description: Defines which Critter monsters can spawn on the area level. Critter monsters are determined by the monstats2.critter field.

columns.name: cmon2
columns.description: Defines which Critter monsters can spawn on the area level. Critter monsters are determined by the monstats2.critter field.

columns.name: cmon3
columns.description: Defines which Critter monsters can spawn on the area level. Critter monsters are determined by the monstats2.critter field.

columns.name: cmon4
columns.description: Defines which Critter monsters can spawn on the area level. Critter monsters are determined by the monstats2.critter field.

columns.name: cpct1
columns.description: Controls the percent chance (out of 100) to spawn a Critter monster on the area level.

columns.name: cpct2
columns.description: Controls the percent chance (out of 100) to spawn a Critter monster on the area level.

columns.name: cpct3
columns.description: Controls the percent chance (out of 100) to spawn a Critter monster on the area level.

columns.name: cpct4
columns.description: Controls the percent chance (out of 100) to spawn a Critter monster on the area level.

columns.name: camt1
columns.description: Controls the amount of Critter monsters to spawn on the area level after they succeeded their random spawn chance from the related .cpct# field.

columns.name: camt2
columns.description: Controls the amount of Critter monsters to spawn on the area level after they succeeded their random spawn chance from the related .cpct# field.

columns.name: camt3
columns.description: Controls the amount of Critter monsters to spawn on the area level after they succeeded their random spawn chance from the related .cpct# field.

columns.name: camt4
columns.description: Controls the amount of Critter monsters to spawn on the area level after they succeeded their random spawn chance from the related .cpct# field.

columns.name: Themes
columns.description: Controls the type of theme when building a level room. This value is a summation of possible values to build a bit mask for determining which themes to use when building a level room. For example, a value of 60 means that the area level can have the following themes: 32, 16, 8, 4.

columns.name: SoundEnv
columns.description: Uses the index of an entry from soundenviron, which controls what music is played while the player is in the area level

columns.name: Waypoint
columns.description: Defines the unique numeric ID for the Waypoint in the area level. If this value is greater than or equal to 255, then ignore this field.

columns.name: LevelName
columns.description: String Field. Used for displaying the name of the area level, such as when in the UI when the Automap is being viewed.

columns.name: LevelWarp
columns.description: String Field. Used displaying the entrance name of the area level on Level Warp tiles that link to this area level. For example, when the player mouse hovers over a tile to warp to the area level, then this string is displayed.

columns.name: LevelEntry
columns.description: String Field. Used for displaying the UI popup title string when the player enters the area level.

columns.name: ObjGrp0
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp1
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp2
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp3
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp4
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp5
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp6
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjGrp7
columns.description: Uses a numeric ID to define which possible Object Groups to spawn in this area level (See objgroup). The game will go through each of these fields, so there can be more than 1 Object Group used in an area level. If this value equals 0, then ignore this.

columns.name: ObjPrb0
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb1
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb2
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb3
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb4
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb5
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb6
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: ObjPrb7
columns.description: Determines the random chance (out of 100) for each Object Group to spawn in the area level. This field depends on the related .ObjGrp# field being defined.

columns.name: LevelGroup
columns.description: Defines what group this level belongs to. Used for condensing level names in desecrated (terror) zones messaging.

columns.name: PreventTownPortal
columns.description: Boolean Field. If equals 1, then prevent town portals from being opened while in this level. If equals 0, then ignore this.
